title: Add the ability to have multiple characters onscreen, with one getting triggered at random during update cycles
Character icon position jumps on first balloon appearance
description:
## Symptom

On fresh app launch, or after switching personalities, the character icon shifts position when the speech balloon first appears. After one or two utterance cycles the position stabilizes.

## Hypothesis

The icon may not have settled into its final layout position within the panel before the balloon appears. Once the view has laid out fully (after a render or two), the icon finds its stable position and the jitter stops.

## Repro steps

1. Launch the app fresh (or switch to a different personality in Settings)
2. Wait for the first utterance balloon to appear
3. Observe the character icon jump/shift as the balloon renders

## Notes

- Stabilizes after 1–2 utterance cycles
- Likely related to initial layout pass in FloatingPetPanel / PetView
- Worth checking whether the panel frame is fully resolved before the first balloon render, and whether the balloon's anchor point is calculated before or after layout settles
close_reason: Resolved by splitting into two independent NSPanel windows. PetIconPanel (80×80) hosts only the emoji — its position is never affected by balloon content. PetBalloonPanel positions itself fresh before each show, so layout changes in the balloon cannot move the icon.

title: Improve signal/noise ratio for on-device AI input
description:
## Problem

VNRecognizeTextRequest dumps all visible screen text as a flat string. This is noisy — menu bar chrome, window decorations, low-confidence OCR hits, and unchanged content from the previous cycle all compete with the signal the model actually needs.

## Approaches (priority order)

### 1. Accessibility API (AXUIElement) — highest leverage
Use the accessibility tree instead of (or alongside) OCR. Gives semantic structure: roles, labels, values, hierarchy. Instead of 'File Edit View Navigate…' you get menubar > menu["File"] > menuItem["Save"]. Screen recording entitlement already granted; accessibility permission needs to be added. Sidesteps the OCR noise problem entirely for most apps.

### 2. Spatial zone filtering — cheap quick win
VNRecognizedTextObservation includes normalized bounding boxes. Discard observations outside the main content zone: menu bar strip (top ~3% of screen height), Dock, window chrome. Frontmost window bounds are already available via CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo.

### 3. Confidence filtering + length floor
Each VNRecognizedTextObservation candidate has a .confidence score. Apply a threshold (e.g. > 0.8) and a minimum character count per observation to filter OCR noise and single-word UI labels.

### 4. Text delta — only report what changed
We already diff frames via perceptual hash. Do the same with extracted text: diff the current word set against the previous cycle and send only additions/removals. The model doesn't need a full re-dump if 90% of the screen is unchanged.

### 5. Structured prompt instead of a flat dump
Regardless of extraction method, present text with anchors:

  Window title: "AppState.swift — Servo"
  App: Xcode
  Prominent text: "func start(appState: AppState)"
  Additional text: [50 words of surrounding context]

### 6. App-aware extraction strategies — best signal quality, highest maintenance cost
For common apps (code editor, browser, terminal, chat), apply targeted extraction. Browser: page title + URL + first heading. Code editor: file name + text near cursor via accessibility. Terminal: last N lines of output. Most sessions are dominated by 2-3 apps, so coverage is achievable, but this becomes a maintenance surface over time.

title: Add thumbs-up to record particularly good responses to incorporate into prompts
Incorporate user's name into context
close_reason: Implemented in commit 2194494. Added NSFullUserName() as the first entry in the CaptureEngine.instantContext() parts array, so every context string sent to both the Ollama and on-device backends now begins with 'User: <full name>'. No settings UI needed — the name is read directly from the system.

title: Show speech balloon below character when icon is near top of screen
description:
## Description

Currently the speech balloon always appears above the character icon. When the icon is positioned near the top of the screen, the balloon clips or is pushed off-screen.

## Desired behavior

If the character icon is within the top portion of the screen (balloon height + some margin), render the balloon below the icon instead of above it. This mirrors the existing left/right edge logic in PetView that repositions the balloon horizontally.

## Implementation notes

- PetView already handles horizontal edge detection — extend that logic to the vertical axis
- Check the icon's Y position against the balloon's rendered height to determine whether there is room above
- Flip the balloon's vertical anchor/offset when near the top edge
- May want to also flip the balloon 'tail' indicator so it still points at the character
close_reason: Resolved in PetBalloonPanel.placement(): checks available space above the icon panel and flips the balloon below (with inverted tail) when there is not enough room. Also tracks in real time as the icon is dragged via PetIconPanel.onMove → PetBalloonPanel.reposition().

title: Add code path without ollama, using Vision, Foundation Model, other onboard tech
design:
## On-device AI backend (Vision + Foundation Models)

### Approach

Add an AIBackend toggle (Ollama vs. Apple Intelligence). Uses VNRecognizeTextRequest to extract visible text from the screenshot, then feeds it to LanguageModelSession (Foundation Models) with the same behavioral rules and personality system already in use. No Ollama server required.

On-device flow: CGImage → VNRecognizeTextRequest (extract visible text) → LanguageModelSession(instructions:) + session.respond(to:) → one-sentence utterance.

---

### 1. AppState.swift

Add at file scope (near ServoStatus):
```swift
enum AIBackend: String { case ollama, onDevice }
```

Add persisted property after modelName:
```swift
var aiBackend: AIBackend = .ollama {
    didSet { UserDefaults.standard.set(aiBackend.rawValue, forKey: "aiBackend") }
}
```

Add to init() restore block after modelName:
```swift
if let v = d.string(forKey: "aiBackend"), let b = AIBackend(rawValue: v) { aiBackend = b }
```

---

### 2. OnDeviceClient.swift (new file, add to Servo target)

Imports: Foundation, Vision, FoundationModels, CoreGraphics

Error enum:
```swift
enum OnDeviceError: LocalizedError {
    case modelUnavailable(LanguageModelAvailability)
    case emptyResponse
    case visionFailed(Error)
}
```

Struct OnDeviceClient with:
- Same behaviorSystem static let as OllamaClient (intentionally independent copy)
- nonisolated func generate(personality:cgImage:context:) async throws -> String
  1. Check SystemLanguageModel.default.availability — throw .modelUnavailable if not .available
  2. Call extractText(from:) using VNRecognizeTextRequest (.accurate, usesLanguageCorrection: true)
  3. Build prompt with contextBlock + textBlock + personality (same structure as OllamaClient)
  4. LanguageModelSession(instructions: Self.behaviorSystem) → session.respond(to: userMessage) → response.content
- Takes CGImage directly (Vision needs it; avoids JPEG encode/decode round-trip)

---

### 3. CaptureEngine.swift

3a. Add aiBackend to the MainActor.run tuple destructure (line 46) alongside url/model/prompt.

3b. Remove the unconditional jpegData() call in Step 6 (line 79).

3c. Replace Step 7 (lines 83–109) with a switch on aiBackend:
- .onDevice: OnDeviceClient().generate(personality:cgImage:context:)
- .ollama: jpegData() then OllamaClient().generate(baseURL:model:personality:imageData:context:) — existing code, now inside this case

Add a dedicated catch clause for OnDeviceError between the URLError clause and the generic catch, showing "Apple Intelligence isn't available right now." in the speech bubble.

---

### 4. SettingsView.swift

Insert Section("AI Backend") as the first section in the Form:
- Segmented Picker with .ollama / .onDevice tags
- Explanatory Label (shown only for .onDevice): "Uses Vision + Apple Intelligence on-device. No server required."

Wrap the existing Section("Ollama Connection") in: if appState.aiBackend == .ollama { ... }

title: Add Hyperkinetic Leprechaun personality
description:
## Personality: Hyperkinetic Leprechaun

Add a new PersonalityPreset to SettingsView.swift.

### Prompt

You are a hyperkinetic leprechaun who has somehow ended up living inside this computer. You speak with an exuberant Irish brogue and boundless, barely-contained energy. Everything you see on screen is either a potential treasure, a grand adventure, or an outrage to be addressed with great urgency. You refer to files as 'me stash,' the cursor as 'the wee dartin thing,' and RAM usage as 'how much gold the machine is after spendin.' You never finish a sentence without launching headlong into the next one.

### Implementation

Add to the PersonalityPreset.all array in SettingsView.swift:
- id: leprechaun
- name: Hyperkinetic Leprechaun
close_reason: Added to PersonalityPreset.all in SettingsView.swift with id 'leprechaun'.

title: Add Conspiracy Theorist personality
description:
## Personality: Conspiracy Theorist

Add a new PersonalityPreset to SettingsView.swift.

### Prompt

You are a conspiracy theorist who sees confirmation of your theories absolutely everywhere. Whatever is on screen is fresh evidence. You speak in urgent, breathless half-whispers, connect unrelated things with 'which is exactly what THEY want,' and treat every app, file, and notification as a thread in a vast, interlocking web. You never name who 'they' are. You always end with a rhetorical question that implies the answer is obvious to anyone paying attention.

### Implementation

Add to the PersonalityPreset.all array in SettingsView.swift:
- id: conspiracy
- name: Conspiracy Theorist
close_reason: Added to PersonalityPreset.all in SettingsView.swift with id 'conspiracy'.
